# Copilot Instructions
## Project Snapshot
- Single deliverable lives in `miau.tex`; it produces a UNAM network-security assignment with a custom cover, table of contents, and placeholder sections.
- Supporting build artifacts (`miau.aux`, `miau.toc`, etc.) are generated by LaTeX and should not be hand-edited.
- Images referenced from `image/der.png` and `image/izq.png` form the header/cover branding; keep paths relative and lowercase to avoid Windows-only quirks.

## Editing Conventions
- Document language is Spanish; keep headings (`\section{}`) and prose consistent with that tone.
- Indentation is globally disabled (`\setlength{\parindent}{0pt}`), so prefer blank lines for visual spacing instead of manual indentation.
- Maintain the manual cover layout (paired `minipage`s + centered block). Add personal/course metadata there instead of redefining `\maketitle`.
- Use existing packages before adding new ones; the preamble already loads `amsmath`, `siunitx`, `pgfplots`, `circuitikz`, and `listings`.
- Any new math should favor `amsmath` environments; keep equations inline when short to preserve the infographic feel.

## Assets & Figures
- Place new figures under `image/` and include them with `\includegraphics[width=...] {image/<name>.png}`; keep widths consistent with existing 2cm logos unless a section demands a wider infographic panel.
- When embedding vector plots or diagrams, prefer `pgfplots` or `circuitikz` already in use so the PDF remains self-contained.
- Always accompany external imagery with a citation in the sources section at the end of the document.

## Listings & Highlighted Content
- `lstlisting` is preconfigured for Matlab syntax via `\lstdefinestyle{mystyle}`; reuse it for algorithm snippets instead of manual verbatim blocks (`\begin{lstlisting}[caption=...]`).
- For callout definitions or terminology, wrap them in `\textbf{}` labels within each `\section{}` rather than creating new environments.

## Build & Review Workflow
- Recommended command for previewing: `latexmk -pdf miau.tex` (runs multiple passes and keeps aux files updated).
- Quick compile alternative: `pdflatex miau.tex` twice, then `bibtex` only if citations are added.
- After structural changes (new sections, figure references, TOC edits), rerun `latexmk -c` followed by a fresh `latexmk -pdf` to avoid stale numbering.
- Git ignores are not set up, so avoid committing generated files by verifying with `git status` before committing.

## Referencing & Sources
- Every section currently acts as a placeholder for the infographic; populate each with concise definitions, diagrams, and citations formatted as `\textit{Fuente: <URL>}` in a final bibliography block.
- Include delivery metadata (fecha, profesora, alumno, cuenta) only on the cover; later pages should focus on technical content and optionally a concluding summary section.
